title: Deconstructed Egg Roll in a Bowl
description: Enjoy all the savory flavors of an egg roll without the fuss of frying! This quick and healthy meal is perfect for a busy weeknight and easily customizable for the whole family.
instructions: In a large skillet or wok, brown the ground pork or turkey over medium-high heat, breaking it apart with a spoon. Drain any excess fat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the shredded carrots and coleslaw mix. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and sriracha (if using).
Pour the sauce over the mixture in the skillet and toss to combine. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Remove from heat and stir in most of the sliced green onions.
Serve immediately in bowls, garnished with remaining green onions and sesame seeds.
tips: For picky eaters, you can serve the meat and vegetable mixture separately, or offer a side of rice or noodles.
To make it even quicker, use pre-minced garlic and ginger.
Adjust the amount of soy sauce and sriracha to your family's taste. For kids, you might want to omit the sriracha entirely.
